questions.question:
Observe the pictures A and B given in Fig. carefully.

Which of the following statement is correct for the above-given pictures?

options_data.option: In A, cars 1 and 2 will come closer and in B, cars 3 and 4 will come closer.
In A, cars 1 and 2 will move away from each other and in B, cars 3 and 4 will move away.
In A, cars 1 and 2 will move away and in B, 3 and 4 will come closer to each other.
In A, cars 1 and 2 will come closer to each other and in B, 3 and 4 will move away from each other.
options_data.explanation: Unlike poles attract each other while like poles repel each other.

questions.question: The arrangement to store two magnets is shown by figures (a), (b), (c), and (d) in Fig.(options). Which one of them is the correct arrangement?

options_data.explanation:
This is the correct arrangement
With time magnets lose their magnetic properties. So, in order to retain their magnetic strength, the pair of magnets are kept with unlike poles in the same direction with a wooden block between them, and metal plates made up of soft iron are placed across the poles as shown in figure (b). This arrangement preserves the magnetic strength of magnets by completing the magnetic circuit.

questions.question:
Three magnets A, B, and C were dipped one by one in a heap of iron filing. Fig. shows the amount of the iron filing sticking to them.

The strength of these magnets will be

options_data.explanation:
A > B > C
The amount of iron fling sticking to magnets is always directly proportional to their strengths.

questions.question: North pole of a magnet can be identified by

options_data.option: Another magnet having marked north and South Pole
options_data.explanation: North pole of a magnet can be identified by another magnet having marked north and south pole. If the test magnet repels by he North pole of another magnet then it is proved true. Since repulsion is the surer test for magnetism.

questions.question: A bar magnet is immersed in a heap of iron filing and pulled out. The amount of iron filling clinging to the

options_data.option: North pole is almost equal to the south pole.
options_data.explanation: North pole is almost equal to the south pole.

options_data.option: North pole is much more than the south pole.
North pole is much less than the south pole.
Magnet will be same all along its length.

questions.question: When a bar magnet is broken; each of the broken parts will have ________ pole/poles.
In a bar magnet, magnetic attraction is ________ near its ends.

Paheli and her friends were decorating the class bulletin board. She dropped the box of stainless steel pins by mistake. She tried to collect the pins using a magnet. She could not succeed. What could be the reason for this?

solution_data.solution: The pins are made of stainless steel which is a non-magnetic material, therefore it magnet will not attract it.

questions.question: How will you test that ‘tea dust’ is not adulterated with iron powder?

solution_data.solution: By using a magnet. If it has iron powder they will stick on to the magnet.

questions.question:
Boojho dipped a bar magnet in a heap of iron filings and pulled it out. He found that iron filings got stuck to the magnet as shown in Fig.

  1. Which regions of the magnet have more iron filings sticking to it?
  2. What are these regions called?

solution_data.solution:
1. The ends of the magnet have more iron flings attached to it.
  2. These regions are called poles of the magnet.

questions.question:
Four identical iron bars were dipped in a heap of iron filings one by one. Fig. shows the amount of iron filings sticking to each of them.

  1. Which of the iron bar is likely to be the strongest magnet?
  2. Which of the iron bars is not a magnet? Justify your answer.

solution_data.solution:
1. Iron bar (a) is likely to be the strongest magnet as the amount of iron filings sticking to it, is much more than other bars.
  2. Iron bar (b) is not a magnet because iron filings do not stick to it.

questions.question: A toy car has a bar magnet laid hidden inside its body along its length. Using another magnet how will you find out which pole of the magnet is facing the front of the car?

solution_data.solution: If the front of the toy car gets attracted to the north pole of the given magnet then it is the south pole of the bar magnet hidden inside the car and vice-versa.

questions.question:
Match Column I with Column II (One option of A can match with more than one option of B)

Column I Column II
(a) Magnet attracts (i) rests along a particular direction
(b) Magnet can be repelled (ii) iron
(c) Magnet if suspended freely (iii) by another magnet
(d) Poles of the magnet can be identified by (iv) iron filings

solution_data.solution: The correct matching order is (a) ii, iii, and iv, (b)-iii, (c)-i, (d)-iii.

questions.question: You are provided with two identical metal bars. One out of the two is a magnet. Suggest two ways to identify the magnet.

solution_data.solution:
There can be the following ways to identify the magnet out of the two identical metal bars:

  1. By suspending the metal bars
  2. By attracting iron filings
  3. Using another magnet

questions.question: Three identical iron bars are kept on a table. Two out of three bars are magnets. In one of the magnets, the North-South poles are marked. How will you find out which of the other two bars is a magnet? Identify the poles of this magnet.

solution_data.solution:
The magnet on which the North-South poles are marked can be used to find the magnet out of two bars.
The magnet with known poles will attract and repel the two ends of a magnet and attend both the end of an ordinary bar. (Test for repulsion). To find out the poles of an unknown magnet, we can use the same test. The north pole marked on the given magnet will repel the north pole of an unknown magnet.

questions.question: Describe the steps involved in magnetising an iron strip with the help of a magnet.

solution_data.solution:
An iron strip can be magnetised by rubbing it with a magnet in a particular direction again and again as shown in figure.

Magnets can also be made with the help of electricity.

questions.question: Fig. shows a magnetic compass. What will happen to the position of its needle if you bring a bar magnet near it? Draw a diagram to show the effect on the needle on bringing the bar magnet near it. Also, draw the diagram to show the effect when the other end of the bar magnet is brought near it

solution_data.solution: The magnetic needle of the compass will get deflected as shown in the figure below:

questions.question: Suggest an activity to prepare a magnetic compass by using an iron needle and a bar magnet.

solution_data.solution: To prepare a magnetic compass, we have to magnetize the given iron needle by rubbing a bar magnet over it repeatedly in a particular direction. After that, it may be kept in such a way so that it can rotate freely when suspended. Hence, the iron needle can act as a compass and give a north-south direction.

questions.question:
Boojho kept a magnet close to an ordinary iron bar. He observed that the iron bar attracts a pin as shown in Fig.

What inference could he draw from this observation? Explain.

solution_data.solution: He could infer from this observation that the magnetic properties are induced into the iron bar and it acts like a magnet till the magnet is kept near it.

questions.question:
A bar magnet is cut into two pieces A and B, from the middle, as shown in Fig.

Will the two pieces act as individual magnets? Mark the poles of these two pieces. Suggest an activity to verify your answer.

solution_data.solution:
Yes, the two pieces A and B will act as individual magnets. A magnet will always have two poles - north and south pole. Each piece will have two poles.

By the test of repulsion, we can conclude that these two pieces A and B will act as individual magnets.

questions.question: Suggest an arrangement to store a U-shaped magnet. How is this different from storing a pair of bar magnets?

solution_data.solution:
U shaped magnet - One metal plate is placed across the two poles of the U shaped magnet to store it.

Bar magnet - Two metal plates and one wooden block is used and arranged as shown in the figure.
